dataset.description: Retinal artery and vein segmentation from fundus images
dataset.acquisition_info: Fundus images captured using ZEISS VISUCAM200 or Canon fundus cameras during real clinical diagnostic processes. Two resolutions: 2656 × 1992 and 1280 × 1280. Images are in PNG format with no compression applied.
dataset.annotation_info: Pixel-wise manual annotation by professional ophthalmologists using 3D Slicer software. Initial annotation by two junior ophthalmologists per image, with uncertain regions reviewed by a senior ophthalmologist. Annotation categories: arteries (red), veins (blue), artery-vein crossings (green), and uncertain vessels (white).
dataset.data_source: Single institution (Shenzhen Eye Hospital) clinical data from 100 patients collected between January and March 2022.
dataset.detailed_description: The Fundus-AVSeg dataset is specifically designed for retinal artery-vein vessel segmentation. It consists of 100 high-resolution fundus images (2656 × 1992 or 1280 × 1280) from 100 patients, including 40 normal cases, 20 diabetic retinopathy cases, 20 age-related macular degeneration cases, and 20 glaucoma cases. Each image has pixel-wise manual annotations distinguishing arteries, veins, artery-vein crossings, and uncertain vessels. The dataset includes both high-quality and intentionally included low-quality images (17% of total) to simulate real clinical conditions.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate segmentation of retinal arteries and veins enables quantitative analysis of vessel morphology, which is associated with systemic chronic diseases including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, glaucoma, and hypertension, providing a basis for early screening and diagnosis.
